title: conium maculatum subsp. leiocarpum
intro: Conium maculatum subsp. leiocarpum, also known as a subspecies of the poisonous hemlock plant, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the carrot family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. The health benefits of Conium maculatum subsp. leiocarpum include relief from pain and swelling, as well as potential uses in treating fever and respiratory issues. Conium maculatum subsp. leiocarpum is often prepared into herbal remedies such as tinctures, infusions, and decoctions, but it's essential to exercise caution when handling the plant due to its toxic nature.
